electroacoustic.directivity: Horizontal: 70 or 110 deg symmetric, or 90 deg asymmetric (35/55 or 55/35) via Panflex adjustable fins, adjustable down to 300 Hz. Vertical: dependent on number of elements and array curvature.
electroacoustic.components: LF: 2 x 12in cone drivers; MF: 4 x 6.5in cone drivers; HF: 2 x 3in diaphragm compression drivers on individual DOSC waveguides. Acoustical load: LF bass-reflex with L-Vents, MF bass-reflex, HF DOSC waveguide with Panflex.
electroacoustic.impedance_detail: LF: 2 x 8 ohm, MF: 8 ohm, HF: 16 ohm (3-way active, 4 input sections)

operating_modes: 3-way active, quad-amplified (4 channels per enclosure: 2x LF, 1x MF, 1x HF)
amplification.amp_channels_required_note: Each K2 requires a full set of 4 amplified channels; per-enclosure counts above reflect how many complete K2 a single controller can drive (LA12X drives 3 K2 across its 4 channels via shared LF, LA7.16i/LA7.16 drive 4, LA4X drives 1).

connection.input_connector: 2 x 8-point PA-COM (1x IN, 1x LINK, interchangeable). PA-COM pinout A/B = left LF, C/D = right LF, E/F = MF, G/H = HF.
connection.breakouts: DO cables (8-point PA-COM, 4 mm2 gauge) for inter-enclosure links; DOSUB-LA8 breakout (8-point PA-COM to 4x 2-point speakON) for passive subs; DOFILL-LA8 and DO3WFILL breakouts for active fill enclosures; SC32-4DO for multi-enclosure distribution.

physical.cabinet: Premium grade Baltic birch plywood; die-cast aluminum side panels; coated steel front grille with acoustically neutral 3D fabric; high-grade steel rigging with anti-corrosion coating; finish dark grey brown Pantone 426 C.

modes: flown with Kara II downfill (via KARADOWNK2)
deployment.max_per_flown_array: K2-BUMP: 16 safe / 24 maximum; K2-LINK under K1-BUMP: 16 safe / 24 max; K2-RIGBAR: 12; pullback (K2-BUMP + K2-BAR or K2-RIGBAR): 12 safe / 16 max; with Kara II downfill (KARADOWNK2): up to 6 Kara II
rigging_accessories: K2-LINK (rear attachment of K2 under K1)
deployment.rigging_system: 4-point captive rigging system with visual safety; two large side handles

usage: Long-throw, full-range line source element for large-scale touring, festivals, and sporting events; built around the K1 acoustic signature in a lighter (56 kg) and more flexible enclosure that delivers up to 147 dB.

Each enclosure is 3-way active and quad-amplified (4 channels: 2 LF, 1 MF, 1 HF), so plan amp inventory accordingly: one LA12X drives 3 K2, one LA7.16i/LA7.16 drives 4 K2, and one LA4X drives a single K2.

Use Panflex to set horizontal coverage per box: 70 or 110 deg symmetric, or 90 deg asymmetric, with a matching preset ([K2 70], [K2 90], [K2 110]) required for each fin setting; coverage settings can be mixed within one array to fit audience geometry.

Pair with subwoofers to extend LF: K1-SB at a recommended 3 K2 : 1 K1-SB ratio (using [K1SB_X K2], no added delay between K2 and K1-SB), or KS28/CS1 at roughly 3 K2 : 2 subs for deeper extension down toward 25 Hz.

Flown arrays use the K2-BUMP (16 safe / 24 maximum K2), or hang under a K1 array via K2-LINK; pullback configurations cap at 12 (safe) / 16 K2 and a Kara II downfill of up to 6 boxes can be added via KARADOWNK2.

Connect with 2x 8-point PA-COM (IN/LINK interchangeable) carrying all four sections; verify the total connected enclosures per controller output stays within limits and always validate array mechanical safety in Soundvision.
